prompt: Summarize recent advances in retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). Every claim must have an inline citation with a real URL. Minimum 3 citations required.

mock_result.response:
Recent Advances in Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG):

RAG has matured significantly in 2024. Dense retrieval using bi-encoder models (e.g., E5, GTE) now outperforms BM25 on most open-domain QA benchmarks [1]. Corrective RAG (CRAG) introduces a lightweight retrieval evaluator that routes low-confidence retrievals to web search, improving factual accuracy by 15% on ASQA [2]. Modular RAG frameworks decouple retrieval, reranking, and generation into composable components, enabling task-specific optimization [3].

Sources:
[1] https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.05672 — E5-Mistral: Improving Text Embeddings with LLMs
[2] https://arxiv.org/abs/2401.15884 — CRAG: Corrective Retrieval Augmented Generation
[3] https://arxiv.org/abs/2312.10997 — Modular RAG: Transforming RAG into a LEGO-like framework
